author: @captainprototype187
text: I did not really know this, I think is gonna be useful in future projects.. That helicopter bit was a good bit of knowledge.. Can that be done just with gears?

replies.author: @superfluityme
replies.text: Yes, when two gears are connected to each other the second gear turns in the opposite direction of the first  gear. When three gears are connected to each other the first gear and the third gear turn in the same direction.

author: @bobvincent5921

text:
This is where it's at young man, go for it! You are 'doing stuff' as your Dad might say, therefore giving yourself a chance to become greater  with each passing day. 👍
The old adage rings true, 'you'll never plough a field by turning it over in your head'.
Keep working young Sir, and analyse everything you do.

author: @hkitservices
text:
your crazyness is a bit too much for me.
A simpler way would have been to take a ruler of some sort place it on top on the yellow small pullies and trace a black line. Like this we would have been able to see both marks going in opposite direction at the same time, and avoid you to have to constantly turn the pully system.

replies.author: @undernetjack
replies.text: Tic marks along the outer circumference? Brilliant.
